research_note: No external coverage found; iceberg-chart lore only.

sources.title: Tulpa - Wikipedia

evidence_against.text: 76.5% of tulpa practitioners in surveys endorse a neurological/psychological explanation; only 8.5% support a metaphysical explanation, undermining the demonic-entity framing.

sources.title: Triad (organised crime) - Wikipedia
Chinese organized crime front serves Beijing's interests - Washington Post

evidence_against.text: Triads are real transnational criminal organizations active in drug trafficking, gambling, and extortion, but no evidence supports covert sovereign global authority over governments.

research_note: No external coverage found; fringe extraterrestrial speculation with no identifiable source community or named proponent.
No external coverage found; iceberg-chart alt-history lore only.
No external coverage found; 'Protein QRD 1451' does not correspond to any known protein designation or suppressed research claim.

sources.title: Inside Quantum Immortality, The Mind-Bending Theory That Suggests Consciousness Never Dies
Quantum Immortality Theory: Living Forever in a Multiverse?

evidence_against.text: Quantum immortality is widely regarded as highly speculative; while the many-worlds interpretation is a real physics framework, there is currently no evidence consciousness migrates between universes at death.

sources.title: The Billionaire Bunkers: Are the Elite Preparing for the End?
Billionaires are building bunkers and buying islands - prepping or pioneering a new feudalism?

evidence_against.text: Analysts argue billionaire bunker construction reflects anxiety and wealth privilege rather than foreknowledge of a coordinated depopulation program; no evidence for intentional environmental destruction as cover for elite retreat.

sources.title: Pyramids Aligned with Stars: Ancient Egyptian Sky Secrets

evidence_against.text: The Orion correlation theory (pyramid-star alignment) is considered fringe; no physical evidence for pyramid alignment with lunar far-side structures exists, and Egyptian texts contain no references to multi-planetary coordinated design.

research_note: No external coverage found; religious-fringe speculation with no identified source community or named proponent.

sources.title: The Charley Project
5 chilling missing persons cases from The Charley Project

evidence_against.text: The Charley Project is a legitimate cold-case missing persons database profiling over 16,000 cases; its sole stated purpose is publicity for neglected cases, with no connection to NPC or soul-tracking theories.

research_note: No external coverage found; fringe biotech/occult speculation with no identifiable source or documented proponent.

sources.title: Sacred geometry - Wikipedia

evidence_for.text: Sacred geometry has documented historical applications in architecture, philosophy, and religion where geometric proportions were used to encode divine meaning; the Golden Ratio appears across natural and human-designed forms.

research_note: No external coverage found; spiritual-fringe lore with no identifiable source community.

sources.title: Noosphere - Wikipedia

evidence_against.text: The noosphere concept (Teilhard de Chardin, Vernadsky) is a philosophical/scientific framework for collective human cognition; the internet-as-demonic-aggregate interpretation has no scholarly or documented basis.

sources.title: Tomoki Uemiya - Kook Science
Unfurling the Cosmos: The Incredible Journey of Tomoki Uemiya

evidence_for.text: Tomoki Uemiya is a documented Japanese contactee/channeler who has published multiple Japanese-language books about extraterrestrial communications since 1994, primarily attributed to Pleiadian sources.

research_note: No external coverage found; fringe elite-alien defection lore with no identifiable source community.

sources.title: The Controversial History Of Ley Lines
Ley lines | Spirituality, Archeology, Origin & Skepticism | Britannica

evidence_against.text: Academic archaeologists do not accept ley line theory; statistical analysis shows alignments are no more meaningful than random chance, and no literature connects nuclear detonations to ley line creation.

sources.title: Psychic driving - Wikipedia
Montreal experiments - Wikipedia
Montreal MKULTRA Experiments | The Canadian Encyclopedia

evidence_for.text: CIA-funded depatterning experiments by Dr. Ewen Cameron at McGill's Allan Memorial Institute (1957-1964) are documented through declassified MKUltra files; patients received massive ECT doses, LSD, and psychic driving, causing lasting harm.
Partial compensation was paid by both the Canadian government and CIA to survivors of Cameron's depatterning program, confirming institutional acknowledgment of the experiments.

sources.title: MKUltra - Wikipedia

evidence_for.text: The base premise that drugs and psychological stress can break down personality for interrogation purposes is substantiated by declassified MKUltra records; the demonic-noosphere extension remains unsubstantiated.

sources.title: Illuminati - Wikipedia
Bavarian Illuminati | History, Secret Societies | Britannica
The Secretive Origins of the Illuminati | HISTORY

evidence_for.text: The historical Bavarian Illuminati (founded 1776 by Weishaupt) explicitly opposed superstition, religious influence on public life, and abuses of state power - consistent with the 'good guys' reading of their stated liberal-Enlightenment goals.

evidence_against.text: The Bavarian Illuminati was dissolved by 1785; no substantiated evidence exists for a continuous modern successor organization operating to benefit the public.

sources.title: Demolition Elves | ObscUrban Legend Wikia
Demolition Elves - Iceberg Database

evidence_for.text: Reports of small humanoid figures at disaster sites have been collected across multiple incidents including 9/11 and the Silver Bridge collapse; Icelandic law formally recognizes elves as entities with territorial rights.

sources.title: Tulpa - Wikipedia
List of conspiracy theories - Wikipedia

evidence_against.text: Fact-checkers consistently debunk staged-war claims; researchers attribute belief in fabricated wars to the broader New World Order conspiracy framework rather than verified supernatural motivations.

sources.title: Proxy war - Wikipedia
Rethinking Proxy Warfare - New America

evidence_for.text: Political science research confirms that nuclear-armed states show increased rates of proxy conflict instead of direct war; nuclear costs foreclose direct hostilities, pushing rivals into surrogate competition.

sources.title: What Is the Fourth Dimension and Why You're Already In It | Gaia

evidence_for.text: Mainstream physics (special relativity) treats time as a 4th dimension, supporting the premise that 4D structure pervades the universe but is not perceivable or freely navigable by ordinary human senses.

sources.title: Astral projection - Wikipedia
Astral Projection, the Astral Body, and the Fourth Dimension - Brotherhood of the White Temple

evidence_against.text: There is no scientific evidence that consciousness can separate from the body and make verifiable observations of the physical universe; astral projection experiences are explained through neurological frameworks.

sources.title: John F. Kennedy assassination conspiracy theories - Wikipedia

evidence_against.text: No variant of a self-inflicted shot appears among the documented JFK conspiracy theories; all catalogued variants involve external assassins, and physical evidence is inconsistent with self-infliction.

sources.title: Moai - Wikipedia
Everything to know about Easter Island's iconic statues | National Geographic

evidence_against.text: Archaeological evidence confirms moai are carved volcanic stone monuments representing Rapa Nui ancestors; they are not fossilized remains. Around 1,000 statues were quarried from Rano Raraku volcano approximately 900 years ago.

sources.title: If the Universe is a Computer Simulation, Can We Hack It? | Gaia
Mathematical proof debunks the idea that the universe is a computer simulation

evidence_against.text: Mathematical analysis using Godel's incompleteness theorem demonstrates the universe cannot be a computer simulation, undermining the premise that numeric patterns can grant reality-manipulation capabilities.

sources.title: Tulpa - Wikipedia
Queer, Toxic Soybeans and Estrogen Panic: Gendered Food Fearmongering
Does Soy Kill Testosterone? The Myth, Debunked

evidence_against.text: Multiple clinical meta-analyses confirm soy isoflavones do not significantly alter testosterone or estrogen levels in men; the soy feminization fear originates from extreme outlier cases and media amplification of misread science.

sources.title: Tulpa - Wikipedia
Prostitution in Tibet - Wikipedia
Traffickers Prey on Tibetan Girls, Women - Radio Free Asia

evidence_for.text: Researchers document that Chinese authorities tacitly condone prostitution in Tibet as a tool of social control, with some sources reporting brothels service occupying Chinese military personnel.

evidence_against.text: No evidence supports the specific claim of a coordinated global political client network; documented exploitation is linked to trafficking, poverty, and Chinese occupation dynamics rather than an organized elite influence operation.

research_note: No external coverage found; iceberg-chart religious-fringe lore only.

sources.title: Fire is Alive Hypothesis: Exposing the Ignis Sapiens
Is Fire Alive? | WFCA

evidence_for.text: Fire meets several informal biological criteria (consumes fuel, produces waste, reproduces, responds to environment); the 'Ignis sapiens' movement argues fire participates in planetary carbon cycling and homeostasis.

evidence_against.text: Biologists classify fire as non-living because it is not composed of cells and lacks genuine biological metabolism; its life-like behaviors are exothermic chemical chain reactions, not biological processes.

sources.title: Prometheus - Wikipedia
Loki, Satan, and Prometheus: Are They Connected? | MysteryLores

evidence_for.text: Scholarly comparison of Prometheus and Lucifer dates to Zvi Werblowsky's 1952 work 'Lucifer and Prometheus'; both figures represent forbidden knowledge and divine rebellion, and some Gnostic traditions identify theft of heavenly fire with the fall of Lucifer.

sources.title: Caloric Restriction in Humans: Impact on Physiological, Psychological, and Behavioral Outcomes - PMC

evidence_for.text: Caloric restriction studies confirm that reducing food intake extends lifespan and delays aging biomarkers across multiple species, providing partial scientific support for a causal link between food consumption rate and aging.

evidence_against.text: Mainstream science attributes aging to multiple independent mechanisms (telomere erosion, oxidative stress, DNA repair failure); food intake is a contributing variable, not the singular cause of aging.

research_note: No external coverage found; iceberg-chart lore only; 'panurgic' is not a recognized term in government or esoteric surveillance research contexts.

sources.title: Investigating The Secrets Of Antarctica: Are Travel Restrictions Covering Up A Global Conspiracy?
Antarctica: The Forbidden Land of Secrets, Conspiracies, and Hidden Truths

evidence_against.text: Travel restrictions are governed by the Antarctic Treaty (signed 1959, ratified 1961), which limits access primarily for environmental protection and scientific preservation, not concealment of hidden civilizations or structures.

research_note: No external coverage found; fringe extension combining hollow earth and 4th dimension frameworks with no identifiable source community.

sources.title: Ghosts in the Machines: The Devices and Daring Mediums That Spoke for the Dead | Collectors Weekly
Late 1800s Fringe Electrotherapeutic Devices: Comparative Electrical Capabilities - PMC

evidence_for.text: Documented Victorian-era fringe electrotherapeutic devices and spiritualist instruments were designed around concepts of animal magnetism, odic force, and ether, confirming that pre-electric technology did develop in directions oriented toward aura/energy frameworks.

sources.title: Noosphere - Wikipedia
Door Dogs | ObscUrban Legend Wikia
Theresa's Haunted History of the Tri-State: Door Dogs

evidence_for.text: Door Dogs are documented in North African (Tangiers, Morocco) folklore as small silent death-omen canines visible to others when a person is near death; William S. Burroughs wrote extensively about this legend in 'The Western Lands', drawing on his time in Tangiers.

sources.title: Tulpa - Wikipedia
